---
title: "Pathway_analysis_RNAseq"
author: "Johannes Schöneich"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

# Libraries and helper functions
```{r libraries}
# Differential-expression object / normalised counts
library(DESeq2)        

# Data wrangling & plotting
library(Hmisc)
library(tidyverse)       
library(reshape2)      
library(glue)            
library(cowplot)       
library(ggpubr)          
library(rstatix)         

# Heatmaps / spreadsheet output
library(ComplexHeatmap) 
library(openxlsx)      

# Pathway / TF activity inference
library(progeny)         # PROGENy model (accessed via decoupleR::get_progeny)
library(decoupleR)       # run_wmean(), get_progeny(), get_collectri()
library(OmnipathR)       # backend for get_collectri()

# Specify the harddrive
harddrive <- "H:/231248/NTFS_3.64TB"

# Custom functions
source(file.path(harddrive, "helper_functions.R"))

# Fixed seed so the permutation-based activity scores are reproducible
set.seed(1234)
```

# Setup: data and output folders

```{r setup-data}
# The condition that was analysed (used in output file names), e.g. timepoint
condition <- "timepoint"

analysis_folder <- "DE_analysis/DE_LRT_uninfected"

rnaseqfile <- "RNA_seq/RNAseq_uninfected_early_late/dds_LRT_RNAseq_uninfected_early_late.Rds"

# DESeq2 object -> library-size-normalised count matrix (genes x samples).
# This matrix is the only input the PROGENy and TF analyses below need.
dds_fullsample <- readRDS(file.path(harddrive, rnaseqfile))
cm.norm <- counts(dds_fullsample, normalized = T)

plots_folder  <- file.path(harddrive, analysis_folder, "Pathway/plots")
charts_folder <- file.path(harddrive, analysis_folder, "Pathway/charts")

dir.create(plots_folder, recursive = T)
dir.create(charts_folder, recursive = T)
```

# PROGENy pathway activity (decoupleR)
```{r progeny-activity}
# PROGENy model for mouse: for each of the 14 pathways, the top 100
# transcriptionally-responsive genes (ranked by p-value), each carrying a signed
# weight = the direction the gene moves when the pathway is active.
net <- get_progeny(organism = 'mouse', top = 100)

# Per-sample pathway activity by weighted mean (WMEAN).
# 'norm_wmean' is the activity score normalised against
# a permutation null (z-score; times = 100 permutations);
# minsize = 5 drops any pathway with < 5 matched target genes.
sample_acts <- run_wmean(mat = cm.norm, net = net, 
                         .source = 'source', .target = 'target',
                         .mor = 'weight', times = 100, minsize = 5)

# Wide matrix: rows = samples, columns = pathways, values = norm_wmean score.
sample_acts_mat <- sample_acts %>%
  filter(statistic == 'norm_wmean') %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = 'condition', 
              names_from = 'source',
              values_from = 'score') %>%
  column_to_rownames('condition') %>%
  as.matrix()

# Absolute (unscaled) activity: active(+) vs repressed(-) per pathway/sample. 
write.xlsx(sample_acts_mat, file = file.path(charts_folder, 
                                             "sample_acts_unscaled.xlsx"), 
           rowNames = TRUE)

# Scale per feature: z-score each pathway (column) across samples. 
# Shows relative dynamics across timepoints
sample_acts_mat <- scale(sample_acts_mat)
sample_acts_mat <- sample_acts_mat[, sort(colnames(sample_acts_mat))]

write.xlsx(sample_acts_mat, file = file.path(charts_folder, 
                                             "sample_acts_scaled.xlsx"), 
           rowNames = TRUE)

# Diverging palette centred at 0; values are clamped to +/-3 for display.
palette_length = 100
my_color = colorRampPalette(c("Darkblue", "white", "red"))(palette_length)

my_breaks <- c(seq(-3, 0, length.out = ceiling(palette_length/2) + 1),
               seq(0.05, 3, length.out = floor(palette_length/2)))

# Heatmap: pathways as rows, samples as columns
# Colors show the scaled (relative) activity.
progeny_heatmap <- pheatmap(t(sample_acts_mat), 
                            border_color = NA,
                            color = my_color,
                            breaks = my_breaks,
                            cluster_rows = FALSE,
                            cluster_cols = FALSE
                            )
```

```{r save progeny heatmap}
png(file.path(plots_folder, "png", glue("progeny_heatmap_{condition}.png")),
    width = 40, height = 20, res = 300)
draw(progeny_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()

pdf(file.path(plots_folder, "pdf", glue("progeny_heatmap_{condition}.pdf")),
    width = 40, height = 20)
draw(progeny_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()
```

# PROGENy activity as boxplots over timepoints
```{r progeny-boxplot-data}
# Long format from the SCALED matrix: one row per pathway x sample.
m_df <- melt(t(sample_acts_mat))

colnames(m_df) <- c("pathway", "timepoint", "value")

# Recover the timepoint from the sample name by dropping the replicate suffix
m_df$timepoint <- str_remove(m_df$timepoint, "_+[1-9]")

m_df$timepoint <- as.factor(m_df$timepoint)
```

```{r progeny-boxplot-filter}
# Keep the pathways relevant to this study and fix their display order.
m_df <- m_df %>% filter(pathway %in% c("JAK-STAT", "MAPK", "PI3K", "TGFb",
                                       "WNT", "Hypoxia", "NFkB", "TNFa"))

m_df$pathway <- droplevels(m_df$pathway)
m_df$pathway <- factor(m_df$pathway, levels = c("JAK-STAT", "MAPK", "PI3K", "TGFb",
                                       "WNT", "Hypoxia", "NFkB", "TNFa"))
```

```{r progeny-anova-tukey}
# One-way ANOVA per pathway (activity ~ timepoint), printed for reference.
pathways <- unique(m_df$pathway)
results <- list()

for (p in pathways) {
  data <- m_df %>% filter(pathway == p) %>% dplyr::select(-pathway)
  result <- aov(value ~ timepoint, data = data)
  results[[p]] <- result
  print(paste("Pathway:", p))
  print(summary(result))
}

# Post-hoc Tukey HSD per pathway
stat.test <- m_df %>%
  group_by(pathway) %>%
  tukey_hsd(value ~ timepoint, p.adjust.method = "BH")
stat.test <- stat.test %>% add_y_position()

# Show ONLY the consecutive-timepoint comparisons; mark every other comparison
# "ns" so it is hidden in the plot (hide.ns = TRUE below).
stat.test[!((stat.test$group1 == "d01" & stat.test$group2 == "d05") |
            (stat.test$group1 == "d05" & stat.test$group2 == "d10") |
            (stat.test$group1 == "d10" & stat.test$group2 == "d25")),
          "p.adj.signif"] <- "ns"

uninf_cols <- c("#E08B00", "#5BB300", "#7997FF", "#FF61C9")
```

```{r progeny-boxplot-plot}
# Faceted boxplot with significance brackets for the consecutive comparisons.
progeny_boxplot <- ggboxplot(m_df, 
                             x = "timepoint", 
                             y = "value",
                             fill = "timepoint",
                             facet.by = "pathway",
                             palette = uninf_cols,
                             nrow = 2,
                             size = 0.1,
                             outlier.size = 0.1) +
  ggtitle(element_blank()) +
  # Optional title:
  # ggtitle(glue("PROGENy pathway analysis {condition}"),
  #         subtitle = "Tukey HSD, consecutive timepoints only (d01/d05,
  #         d05/d10, d10/d25)") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", axis.title = element_blank(),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, 
                                   vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1, size = 7),
        strip.text = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(), 
        strip.background = element_blank(),
          legend.background = element_blank(),
          panel.background = element_blank(), # transparent panel bg
          plot.background = element_blank(),  # transparent plot bg
        aspect.ratio = 1
        ) + 
  stat_pvalue_manual(stat.test, label = "p.adj.signif", tip.length = 0.01, hide.ns = TRUE) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.1)))
```

```{r save-progeny-boxplot-formats}
save_ggplot_formats(
  plt = progeny_boxplot,
  create_plot_subdir = T,
  base_plot_dir = plots_folder,
  plt_name = glue("PROGENy_boxplot_{condition}_full_v5"),
  width = 6, height = 4
)
```

# Transcription factor activity (decoupleR + CollecTRI)
```{r decoupler transcription factor analysis}
# CollecTRI mouse regulons: signed TF-target interactions, where the mor column
# gives the direction of regulation. split_complexes = TRUE separates protein
# complexes into their individual members.
net_tf <- get_collectri(organism = 'mouse', split_complexes = TRUE)
net_tf

# Per-sample TF activity by weighted mean (500 permutations for the null).
sample_acts_tf <- run_wmean(mat = cm.norm, net = net_tf, .source = 'source', .target = 'target',.mor = 'mor', times = 500, minsize = 5)

n_tfs <- 100

# Wide matrix: rows = samples, columns = TFs, values = norm_wmean score.
sample_acts_tf_mat <- sample_acts_tf %>%
  filter(statistic == 'norm_wmean') %>%
  pivot_wider(id_cols = 'condition', names_from = 'source',
              values_from = 'score') %>%
  column_to_rownames('condition') %>%
  as.matrix()

# Select the most dynamic TFs: those with the highest SD of activity across samples.
tfs <- sample_acts_tf %>%
  group_by(source) %>%
  summarise(std = sd(score)) %>%
  arrange(-abs(std)) %>%
  head(n_tfs) %>%
  pull(source)

sample_acts_tf_mat <- sample_acts_tf_mat[, tfs]

# Scale per feature: z-score each TF (column) across samples (relative dynamics).
sample_acts_tf_mat <- scale(sample_acts_tf_mat)

# Choose color palette
palette_length = 100
my_color = colorRampPalette(c("darkblue", "white", "red"))(palette_length)

my_breaks <- c(seq(-3, 0, length.out = ceiling(palette_length/2) + 1),
               seq(0.05, 3, length.out = floor(palette_length/2)))

# Heatmap: TFs as rows, samples as columns, no clustering.
tf_heatmap <- pheatmap(t(sample_acts_tf_mat), border_color = NA,
                       cluster_rows = FALSE, cluster_cols = FALSE, color = my_color)
```

```{r Save data}
png(file.path(plots_folder, "png", glue("progeny_transcription_factor_heatmap_{condition}.png")),
    width = 20, height = 40, res = 300)
draw(tf_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()

pdf(file.path(plots_folder, "pdf", glue("progeny_transcription_factor_heatmap_{condition}.pdf")),
    width = 20, height = 40)
draw(tf_heatmap, heatmap_legend_side = "left")
dev.off()
```
